texto: When the jīva, reflected consciousness, has its inert association with the body totally destroyed and ignited by the fire of jñāna, it burns in the huge and extensive cremation ground, the cidākāśa. The vision of this excellent effulgence is similar to the sight of an unbounded conflagration that rages when a vast forest, dense with dried trees, catches fire and spreads in all directions.
explicacao: Citação de Muruganar sobre temas ligados a autoestima. “When the jīva, reflected consciousness, has its inert association with the body totally destroyed…” articula autoestima, self-realization, vedanta em registro de autoestima, com relevância para contextos como vedanta.
